summary: I came to the United States from China when I was fifteen because my parents wanted me to have better political and economic opportunities. When I first arrived, I couldn't speak English and it was very hard to adjust, but over time I got used to living here. I was the first in my family to attend college, studying at the University of Hawaii, and now I have been a teacher for over 15 years. America has given me a fresh start and the chance to build a loving family and a fulfilling life.
cues: first in family to attend college

summary: I was born and raised in China before coming to the United States as a teenager. My parents wanted me to leave China because they felt there were limited political and economic opportunities there. I still visit China often and notice how much it has changed with new technology and opportunities, though people still face restrictions on their rights. I sometimes wonder what my life would have been like if I had stayed, but I am proud to be Chinese American.

I attended the University of Hawaii after coming to the United States, and it was a crucial part of my journey. My teachers there were very patient and helped me a lot when I was still struggling with English. Being the first person in my family to go to college was a big achievement, and the education I received helped me become a teacher. The University of Hawaii gave me the support I needed to succeed in a new country.
cues: first in family to attend college
